free_text: A study of the reactions occurring in 16O+159Tb system:
measurement of excitation functions and recoil range
distributions
Department of physics, DSN College, UNNAO.
The spectroscopically pure self-supporting foils of
159Tb (purity 99.99%) were rolled in order to obtain a
desired thickness of samples. The measured thicknesses
of the 159Tb foils were 1.8 mg/cm2.
The Al-holders were used for rapid heat dissipation.
The Al-degraders kept between two successive 159-Tb
samples served both as energy degrader as well as
catcher foils.
The beam of oxygen ions of charge state
Two stacks containing four 159-Tb samples
each, were irradiated by 16-O(7+) beam at 90 and
95 MeV.
The irradiations were carried out for 8 hours
duration each. The beam currents were 50-nA.
The delay time between the stop of irradiation
and the beginning of counting was minimized using
in-vacuum transfer of samples from the scattering
chamber to the counting system.
In an auxiliary experiment the flux of the
incident beam determined from the charge collected in
the Faraday cup was compared with that calculated from
the Rutherford monitors held at +/-30 degrees with
respect to the beam direction. The two readings of the
flux agreed with each other within the uncertainty of
about 5%.
